messages.content: Bob is a popular student who has displayed leadership qualities in our class projects. Bob’s performance in class has seen some improvement, but it is believed that with continued effort and dedication, he can continue to grow and excel. Bob’s speaking skills still require additional work. I suggest reading out loud at home. I also hope that Bob can take time to  On occasion, Bob becomes sidetracked by classmates, but the teacher would like him to focus more and keep his work organized and on schedule. By doing so, Bob could improve his concentration and grades. Overall, the teacher sees Bob’s potential and looks forward to seeing him again in the next semester.

Bob is a promising student with excellent leadership qualities, but there is still room for improvement in his academic performance and speaking skills. To enhance his speaking skills, the teacher suggests reading aloud at home. Additionally, the teacher expects Bob to focus more on his work and to keep his work organized and on schedule, allowing him to improve his concentration and attain better grades. Despite these challenges, the teacher believes in Bob's potential and looks forward to seeing his continued growth and academic excellence in the next semester.

Paraphrase this: 

Bruce is a good example to the other students in his behaviour and attitude. He always makes a big effort to complete the class work and homework carefully. Bruce remembers concepts and vocabulary well but he has great difficulty when it comes to spelling. Although he has exhibited an exhaustive grasp of the English topics we have been studying, he sometimes can be overconfident and miss points rather than look up the information to confirm his theories. Bruce should be happy with his progress and understanding of the lesson content as he is doing very well for the most part. His pronunciation is very good. I am impressed with Bruce's reading ability. He tries very hard, finishes his class work and he remembers most new words and phrases. He does lose important papers from time to time. I think this could be improved for next semester! All in all, he is an excellent student that I can often rely on to answer questions in class as well. Great work Bruce!

Bruce is a role model for his peers due to his positive attitude and exemplary conduct. He displays commitment towards completing classwork and homework accurately, and has an impressive memory for concepts and vocabulary. Notably, however, he struggles with spelling and can sometimes overestimate his knowledge, leading to miss marks. Despite these challenges, Bruce should take pride in his accomplishments, as he is performing exceptionally in most areas. His pronunciation is particularly remarkable, and his reading skills are impressive. While he could improve on his habit of losing important papers, Bruce is an outstanding student who consistently participates in class discussions. Overall, he is a reliable and dedicated learner, and his efforts are truly commendable.

Please paraphrase this: 
Kitty is one of the best students in her level. She takes pride in the work that she does and is very proactive in class. Kitty has demonstrated a key grasp of the English topics we have covered in class. She learns new material quickly and is making great progress. I am always impressed with Kitty's reading ability, especially her recognition of the common vocabulary words. I would like Kitty to practice more descriptive sentences in writing, spelling and tenses. Kitty is progressing at a very rapid pace, but sometimes she can spend a lot of time doubting herself. I hope she can have more confidence in her spoken English and do her best. She has the potential to do great things. I’m really looking forward to seeing her progress next semester!

Kitty stands out as one of the top-performing students in her class by displaying a strong work ethic and taking a proactive approach to learning. She has shown significant mastery of the English topics covered in class, being quick to grasp new concepts and making impressive strides in her progress. In particular, Kitty's reading skills are noteworthy, particularly in her ability to identify and use standard vocabulary words. However, it is suggested that she works on developing her descriptive language skills and improving her spelling and tense usage in writing. While Kitty is making remarkable progress, she tends to doubt herself at times, which hinders her success. The teacher hopes that Kitty will be more confident in her spoken English and make the most of her potential. The teacher looks forward to observing Kitty's continued growth and academic accomplishments in the next semester.

Paraphrase this: 
Toby has a strong grasp on the English language and her abilities reflect that. Toby is a very well-behaved student. She has demonstrated a full grasp of the English topics we have been studying. She seems to grasp new materials quickly and is able to demonstrate new vocabulary and grammar in a practical manner. Toby remembers new words and reads very well. She gets along well with her classmates and has made a good friends with other members of the class. Toby has some trouble with grammar when writing but I think this could be fixed if she read more in English. All in all, she is an absolute joy to have in class and I’m looking forward to teaching her again next semester!#

Toby is a highly skilled English speaker, reflecting her solid understanding of the language. She consistently conducts herself well in class and impresses her teacher with her intelligence and proficiency in the topics covered. Toby's ability to quickly grasp new material and effectively utilize relevant vocabulary and grammar is commendable. Additionally, she boasts strong reading skills and maintains positive relationships with her classmates. While Toby struggles with grammar in her writing, it is suggested that she can improve this by reading more in English. Overall, Toby's presence in class is delightful, and the teacher is eager to teach her again in the next semester.

Paraphrase this: 
Ryan is a very quiet but clever student. He gets along well with the other students in the class and his understanding of the lesson material is level appropriate. Ryan has demonstrated a basic understanding of the English material we have covered in class but has difficulty when it comes to writing. His grammar is improving but I have told him now to improve his handwriting and punctuation as these are weak areas. In speaking projects, Ryan is quite shy and will speak very quietly making it difficult to understand the messages he is trying to convey. It would benefit Ryan a great deal if he would speak louder and clearer not only in projects but also in class. Having more confidence would benefit anyone and it will especially benefit Ryan. He has great potential and I’m sure speaking more in English or reading more will boost his confidence. All in all, Ryan is a wonderful student and I very much look forward to seeing him in class again next semester!

Ryan is a reserved yet intellectually perceptive student who interacts well with his classmates. His proficiency level appears adequate for the class. While he has demonstrated a rudimentary understanding of the English material covered, he struggles with writing and needs to improve his handwriting and punctuation skills, as these are areas of weakness. When it comes to oral communication, Ryan tends to be timid and often speaks softly, hindering clarity of expression. It is recommended that he develop a more confident and assertive speaking style, which will benefit him in both class and projects. Building confidence through activities such as reading or speaking more in English will help unlock his true potential. Overall, Ryan is a delightful student, and the teacher eagerly anticipates having him back in class in the upcoming semester.

paraphrase this: 
Niko is a good kind student that is respectful to his teachers and fellow students. He gets along with classmates and enjoys reading. Unfortunately, as of lately, Niko has been reading Chinese books in class and has become increasingly uninterested in class material. In addition to this, he is not very proactive in class. There has been some occasion where Niko has forgotten to bring the proper materials to class. Either books, pens or notebooks. Niko has proven to have a key understanding of the English topics we have covered in class. He mostly follows along well although he sometimes gets a little confused by new words or ideas. He always double checks his understanding with the teacher. Niko is able to read at an appropriate level for this class/ I think that once Niko becomes less shy and more self confident he will speak more in class and improve his speaking exponentially. Niko is a very clever student that has great potential in English. I know that if he focuses more on his work, he will improve. I believe in him! I hope he continues to stay focused on his English goals! It’s important to note that Niko is one of the students I often call on in class to answer questions in class. He reliably knows the answers! I am looking forward to seeing Niko again in class next semester.

Niko is a considerate and respectful student who maintains good relationships with his teachers and peers. He is also an avid reader, but recently, he has been reading Chinese books in class, thus diverting his attention from the class material. In addition, Niko lacks proactiveness in class and forgets to bring essential class materials, including textbooks, pens, and notebooks. Despite these drawbacks, Niko demonstrates a fair understanding of the English topics covered in class, although he may get perplexed by unfamiliar words or concepts. Nevertheless, he conscientiously seeks clarification from the teacher, and his reading skills are appropriate for the class level. To unlock his potential, it is suggested that he builds more confidence to speak up in class, which will also improve his speaking skills. The teacher believes that Niko is an intelligent student with great potential in English, and if he focuses more on his work, he will improve. Furthermore, Niko is a reliable student who frequently answers questions in class. The teacher is excited to have him back in class in the upcoming semester and hopes he remains committed to his English goals.

Paraphrase this: 
Gavin has shown a marked improvement from the first unit. He is beginning to grasp the impact of his grades in relation to his study. He gets along with classmates a little better now and spends more time in class activities rather than be distracted by his friends. All in all, Gavin has exhibited some grasp of the English topics we have covered in class. He demonstrates a better comprehension of the materials we are learning in class. He can correctly use basic verb tenses in short sentences but I would like him to start speaking in longer, more accurate sentences. Gavin finds English challenging but is making good progress. With his latest scores seeing marked improvement, I’m sure that he can continue to improve by focusing on his weak areas. Gavin has great potential and I’m sure that with time he will make good progress.

Gavin has made an evident improvement since the first unit, as he begins to understand the significance of his grades in his academic studies. He now relates better with his classmates and reduces time spent being distracted by friends during class activities. Gavin has demonstrated a basic understanding of the English topics covered in class, showing better comprehension of relevant materials. Additionally, he can accurately use basic verb tenses in short sentences, although he needs to work towards constructing more extended and accurate sentences while speaking. Although Gavin finds English challenging, he has made commendable progress, with his latest scores showing a marked improvement. It is expected that he can continue to enhance his skills by focusing on identified areas of weakness, given his clear potential. The teacher is optimistic that with time, Gavin will make significant strides in his language proficiency.

Paraphrase this: 
Raiden always tries to help other students; thank you Raiden! He is not afraid to speak up in class and he gets along well with the other students. Raiden has demonstrated an increasing range of topics we have covered in class, but he still struggles with the content. Raiden has difficulty with some points in class due to his organization. He often loses papers that have been handed out in class. I hope that Raiden will pay more attention, keep his papers safe and focus on his reading and writing skills in the future. Raiden’s spoken English has great potential, and if he prepared more and followed directions, he could get much higher scores. Raiden just needs more focus to do this. All in all, Raiden has a great potential and I’m sure he can do better if he focus his efforts. All in all, Raiden is a joy to have in class and I’m very much looking forward to seeing him again next semester.

Raiden is a kind and helpful student who is not hesitant to contribute in class. He has a cordial relationship with his peers, and his skills are gradually improving in various class topics despite facing some difficulties. However, his poor organization and tendency to lose papers have compromised his performance in some class areas. Therefore, it is recommended that Raiden becomes more mindful and conscious of his organizational skills while focusing on developing his reading and writing skills in the future. Notably, Raiden has the potential for excellent spoken English, but he needs to take preparation and follow instructions to achieve higher scores. With improved attention to detail and focus on his studies, Raiden can unlock his true potential. Overall, he is an asset to the class, and the teacher eagerly awaits his return next semester.

Paraphrase this: 
Jeana is a friendly, cheerful student who gets along well with the other students in the class and completes the class exercises conscientiously. Jeana has demonstrated a grasp of the topics we have been studying but unfortuntately has difficulty when it comes to speaking. She has trouble with pronunciation, intonation and volume. All of this makes it difficult to understand her in speaking projects. I really suggest that Jeana practice reading outloud at home to improve in this area. Her writing skill is very good but her speaking needs improvement. I believe that if Jeana had more confidence, she would be more likely to practice in class. Reading more at home and speaking more in English could help in this area. At the moment, Jeana is quiet and reserved. She has amazing potential though and her last writing task was completed very well! I am looking forward to seeing her improvements next semester!

Jeana is an amiable, affable student who relates well with her peers and diligently completes class exercises. While she demonstrates a fair understanding of the topics covered, she struggles when it comes to speaking, with difficulties in pronunciation, intonation, and volume, making it hard to comprehend her during speaking projects. To improve this aspect of her learning, it is suggested that Jeana practices reading aloud at home. Her writing skills are commendable, but there is much room for enhancement regarding her speaking. If Jeana could cultivate more confidence, she would be more inclined to exercise her speaking skills in class. Reading more English texts at home and conversing more would also aid in this pursuit. Currently, Jeana is a reticent student, but her potential for remarkable progress is evident, demonstrated by her excellently completed last writing task. The teacher looks forward to witnessing her advancements further in the upcoming semester.

Darren has a good general understanding of the materials we have studied until now but has some difficulty with more complex words, I am pleased with his reading progress. He listens attentively but can sometimes be distracted by his classmates. Darren unfortunately has a tendency to miss place papers that have been handed out in class. It’s not worthy of his abilities if he loses points for directions. With a little more effort on his part I am sure Darren will have much success in his future English studies. He has a great potential!

David is a quiet and friendly student. He is a very thoughtful and respectful to the teacher. Unfortunately, David has some difficulty with grammar, spelling, and bringing the proper materials to class such as project papers. He often needs replacement worksheets as well. I would recommend that David organize his papers so that he doesn’t have this problem. All in all, I have begun to see a strong improvement in David’s work lately. I can see an upward trend and I hope it continues into the future. In order for David to improve his grammar and spelling, I would like to recommend that David read more books at his level. There’s a series here in the school that is quite popular and easy. It teaches new vocabulary with every topic. I will give David a few for the winter break so that he can practice at home. All in all, David is wonderful and I enjoy having him in class! He is an important part of our 7B7 class and I look forward to seeing him again next semester.
